name: White Chocolate-Hazelnut Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F and position a rack in the center.
2. Lightly butter baking sheets.
3. In a double boiler over hot (not simmering) water, melt the butter and 8 ounces of white chocolate, stirring frequently. Set aside until tepid.
4. In a large bowl, beat eggs until light and frothy. Beat in sugars until the mixture is light and fluffy, about 3 minutes.
5. Blend in the tepid white chocolate mixture and vanilla.
6. In a double boiler over simmering water, melt cream cheese, stirring occasionally. Blend into the white chocolate mixture.
7. In a medium bowl, combine flour, baking soda, and cinnamon; blend into the white chocolate mixture.
8. Fold in the remaining white chocolate, semisweet chocolate, and hazelnuts.
9. Spoon heaping tablespoonfuls, two inches apart, onto prepared pans.
10. Bake until golden brown and firm to touch, about 8-10 minutes.
11. Transfer cookies to wire racks and cool completely.
12. Store in an airtight container at room temperature.
